void.$description: Void — Page canvas, hero background, footer background — the absolute ground everything else sits on or within

carbon.$description: Carbon — Subtle surface variation from the page canvas — used as a near-identical lifted surface where one is needed without breaking the monochrome spell

ash.$description: Ash — Secondary text, muted labels, inactive input borders — a half-step between white and black for elements that must recede

bone.$description: Bone — Primary text, hairline rules, link and button borders, image borders — the only forward-facing tone, used with restraint as both type and geometry

neue-haas-grotesk-tp-55-roman.$description: Sole typeface for all interface text — navigation, body, headlines, buttons, footer. The exclusive use of weight 400 is a deliberate anti-hierarchy choice; scale and spacing do the work that weight normally would. The OpenType 'case' feature is enabled site-wide, giving all-caps small text properly designed uppercase parentheses, hyphens, and numerals instead of lowercase glyphs scaled up.

void.$description: Surface level 0: Base page canvas — every screen begins here

carbon.$description: Surface level 1: Near-identical lifted surface for the rare case where a region must differ from the base without breaking the monochrome field

ash.$description: Surface level 2: Stepped-back typographic surface — secondary text and inactive input borders that need to read but not compete

bone.$description: Surface level 3: Forward-facing surface — primary text, hairline rules, and the 1px frame around imagery and bordered chips
